data.content:
The problem is classic: the `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. Globs are evaluated at project load time, before the target runs, so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` (and `<FileWrites>` for clean support) inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is created in `$(IntermediateOutputPath)Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but MSBuild's glob for `Compile` items has already been evaluated at project load time, so it won't pick up files created during the build.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after it's been created. This is the classic "including generated files" pattern.

Additionally, it should be added to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.


Now I'm updating the .csproj to include the generated file in the Compile items after creation and register it for cleanup.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the `obj/` directory but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild evaluates glob patterns (implicit `<Compile Include="**/*.cs" />`) at project load time — before any targets execute — so the newly created file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..." />` and `<FileWrites Include="..." />` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` after the file is written. This explicitly tells the compiler about the generated file and registers it for cleanup on `dotnet clean`.
